chief_complaint: A 1-year-old toddler presented with a 1-day history of persistent non-bilious vomiting, intermittent pallor, and irritability.
history: The patient had a 1-day history of persistent non-bilious vomiting, intermittent pallor, and irritability, without fever or diarrhoea. Initial examination was non-specific, but progressive abdominal tenderness and new onset of fever prompted imaging and laboratory investigations.
exam: Progressive abdominal tenderness and new onset of fever.
labs: Elevated inflammatory markers, serum lipase, and contrast-enhanced CT findings.
